disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Infant has a known, unrepaired cleft lip and cleft palate and feeds with special cleft bottles
trigger_question_examples: Tell me about his health history.
Does he have any medical conditions or feeding problems?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He was born with a cleft lip and cleft palate that haven't been fixed yet — he feeds with special bottles and is under the cleft team.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Total refusal of feeds for 24 hours with crying and pushing the bottle away
trigger_question_examples: How has his feeding been?
When did the feeding trouble start?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: For a whole day now he screams and pushes the bottle away and won't drink anything.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Milk regurgitates through the nose during feeding
trigger_question_examples: What happens when you try to feed him?
Does the milk come back up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: When we manage to get any in, the milk comes straight back out of his nose.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High fever developed approximately 6 hours ago
trigger_question_examples: Has he had a fever?
Does he feel hot?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, he started burning up about 6 hours ago — our thermometer read 39.1.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No wet nappy for about 14 hours (markedly reduced urine output)
trigger_question_examples: When did he last have a wet nappy?
How many wet nappies has he had today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: That's what scared us — nothing since yesterday afternoon, about 14 hours, all his nappies are bone dry.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Infant is lethargic and floppy with a weak, hoarse cry (reduced responsiveness)
trigger_question_examples: How alert is he compared with normal?
Is he responding to you as usual?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's gone really floppy and sleepy and his cry is weak and hoarse — he's just not himself.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Skin looks mottled/blotchy and the infant feels hot to touch
trigger_question_examples: What does his skin colour look like?
Is he pale or blotchy anywhere?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His skin's gone all blotchy and mottled and he feels really hot.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No tears when crying and dry, sticky mouth
trigger_question_examples: When he cries, are there tears?
Does his mouth look moist or dry?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No tears at all when he cries, and his little mouth looks dry and sticky.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Infant appears to be in pain, especially when trying to suck/feed
trigger_question_examples: Does he seem to be in pain?
Is he tugging at his ears or crying as if something hurts when he sucks?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He seems to be in real pain when he sucks — he pulls off and screams almost straight away.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Infant paracetamol was given about 4 hours ago with minimal effect
trigger_question_examples: Have you given him anything for the fever or pain?
What medicines has he had today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: We gave him infant paracetamol about 4 hours ago but it barely touched it.

trigger_question_examples: Has he been vomiting?
Any diarrhoea or loose nappies?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, he hasn't vomited and there's been no diarrhoea — the nappies are just dry, not dirty.
